mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present values and supporting S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for FEDFUNDS vs 20-day returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in long-only backtest when FEDFUNDS is below its 10th percentile.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5% indicates significant labor market slack, reducing aggregate demand and corporate profits, which depresses equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If in periods when UNRATE exceeds 5%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is non-negative over any 5-year sample, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of near-zero interest rates and elevated unemployment signals a severe economic downturn with deflationary pressures, leading to negative equity returns as markets price in prolonged weakness.
failure_condition: If in periods satisfying the trigger condition, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is above 100 and exhibiting positive momentum, the economy is in a supply-led expansion. Rising output boosts corporate revenues and earnings, driving equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: supply-side expansion: INDPRO > 100 and 3-month change positive
failure_condition: If during regime-defined periods, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p < 0.10) over the available sample, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales reflect strong consumer spending, a primary driver of GDP growth. Robust demand signals healthy corporate earnings, supporting short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-positive over any 2-year period, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price levels above 260 (Index 1982-1984=100) indicate elevated inflation that pressures central banks to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and lowering equity valuations over the near term.
failure_condition: If in periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year window, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment is high and unemployment remains elevated, it suggests structural labor market mismatches or discouraged worker effects, weakening the economic outlook and leading to negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are non-negative in a majority of periods satisfying the trigger over any 5-year sample, hypothesis rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts above 1,400 thousand units signals a housing recovery that spurs construction, employment, and consumer spending, providing a positive impulse to equities over the following month.
regime_hint: housing-led recovery: HOUST rose at least 5% from a 3-month low and >1400
failure_condition: If during housing-led recovery regimes the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p < 0.05) over the available sample, hypothesis rejected.
